evidence_quotes.quote: "perpetual" licenses for software and media, only for them to be revoked/disabled a few years later.
If a publisher can freely take away our digital goods, then there's no such thing as digital fairness.

paragraphs.text: Europeans are being sold "perpetual" licenses for software and media, only for them to be revoked/disabled a few years later. There's countless cases of this happening with productivity tools, video games, films, TV shows, and so on. Even hardware is being disabled at a firmware level, turning functional electronics into e-waste once the manufacturer ends their support. It's causing financial harm to consumers and businesses alike, with some licenses costing 3,000+ per seat. And for creative works that are no longer published, it's comparable to book burning. I urge the Commission to address this. If a publisher can freely take away our digital goods, then there's no such thing as digital fairness.
